name: Ripple

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Dependency and CSS hygiene from the directory validation: the builtin-modules package is replaced by Node's own module list, and the composer no longer uses a :has selector. Assets carry build-provenance attestations.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
First release: a micro-journal for Obsidian in the spirit of [Pile](https://github.com/UdaraJay/pile) by Udara Jay.

## Highlights

- **One linked folder of plain Markdown** (default `Ripple/`, changeable): posts land as timestamped notes with a small frontmatter block; files dropped in by any other route appear in the feed — the folder is the API.
- **Feed and composer**: newest-first under day headers, in-place editing, keyboard-first (`j`/`k`, `Enter`, `r`, `h`, `t`, `o`, `n`), a month scrubber on the pane's edge, and a gentle settle-in on open.
- **Threads**: replies nest under posts via `reply_to` wikilinks; renames never orphan a conversation.
- **Highlights**: six theme-safe colours, shown as the post's ball on the rail, filterable from the navigation.
- **Name note**: opt-in human filenames per post (`t` or the post menu), suggested from the first sentence, renamed through Obsidian's link-aware machinery.
- **AI reflections (optional)**: streamed threaded replies via the [AI Providers](https://github.com/pfrankov/obsidian-ai-providers) plugin — Ripple holds no API keys and talks to no provider directly; a reflection is written only on completion, and stopping one writes nothing. Everything else works without AI Providers installed.
- **Navigation choreography**: opening Ripple swaps the left sidebar to its own navigation (timeline, months, highlights, tags) and gives the feed a full-bleed pane; closing restores your layout.

Ripple is an independent project inspired by Pile and is not affiliated with or endorsed by Pile or Udara Jay.
